title: stack-graph handbook
read-when: Orienting to stack-graph — what it is and how the handbook is organised.

title: Authoring handbook pages
read-when: Writing or editing a stack-graph handbook page.

title: Maintenance — the forcing rule
read-when: You hit drift, ambiguity, or a missing page while working in stack-graph.

title: Concepts
read-when: You need the shared mental model before reading any spec.

title: Graph specification
read-when: Defining or implementing the node/edge model, the node schema, or storage.

title: Plugin specification
read-when: Packaging the plugin or building the handbook→plugin pipeline.

title: Harness specification
read-when: Defining how a consuming workspace customises the vendored graph.

title: Maintenance skill (stack-graph-maintainer)
read-when: Specifying or building the skill that authors and maintains the graph.

title: Analytics & instrumentation
read-when: Specifying how usage and workflow-conformance are measured.

title: Decomposition principles
read-when: Deciding how to break a process into nodes, edges, and inline elements.

title: DevOps — how stack-graph develops itself
read-when: Working on stack-graph itself — branching, PRs, the improvement loops.
